name: Mavenlink (Kantata) Professional Services Automation API
what_it_does: Professional services automation (PSA) platform (rebranded as Kantata) with REST API — enabling professional services organizations to manage project lifecycle from sales opportunity to revenue recognition, including resource planning, project financials, time and expense tracking, capacity management, and business intelligence for services P&L optimization.
use_cases: Syncing Kantata project financial data with ERP systems (NetSuite, Salesforce) via API
Querying resource availability and utilization metrics via Kantata API for workforce planning
Managing project budgets, actuals, and revenue forecasts via Kantata API for financial reporting
Integrating Kantata time and expense data with payroll and billing systems via API
Querying project health scores and delivery risk signals via Kantata API for executive dashboards
not_for: General task management without financial tracking (use Asana for simple task management)
Small agencies (Kantata/Mavenlink targets 50+ person professional services firms)
Software product teams without billable hours model (Kantata is services-centric)
best_when: A mid-to-large professional services firm (consulting, IT services, marketing agency) needs to connect sales pipeline, resource management, project delivery, and revenue recognition in a single system to optimize services P&L.
avoid_when: You're a small agency, don't have complex resource planning needs, primarily do fixed-price projects without hourly billing, or are a product company rather than services firm.

auth.notes: OAuth2 authorization code and client credentials flows. Bearer token auth. Account-scoped with role-based access. Webhook for project, resource, and financial events. REST API for PSA workflow automation. Salesforce certified integration for CRM-to-PSA pipeline. NetSuite integration for financial sync. SAML SSO for enterprise identity. Scoped by resource type (projects, resources, financials, timesheets).

pricing.paid_tiers: Enterprise pricing; contact sales; typically $50-150/user/month based on modules
pricing.notes: San Francisco company. Founded 2008 (as Mavenlink). Rebranded to Kantata in 2022 after merger with Kimble Applications (UK-based PSA). $35M+ raised. Backed by Alibaba Growth Equity, Lead Edge Capital. 2,000+ professional services customers. Gartner Magic Quadrant for Professional Services Automation. Strong in IT consulting, engineering services, marketing agencies. Kimble merger expanded to UK/EU market. Salesforce ISV partner with AppExchange presence. BI/analytics layer (Kantata OX) for services analytics.

performance.rate_limits: Enterprise rate limits per account; contact support

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for PSA workflow automation. OAuth2 client credentials auth. Good documentation with Salesforce/NetSuite integration guides. Webhook for project and financial events. Kantata rebrand from Mavenlink means documentation may reference both names. Kimble merger means two historical API versions — verify active API version. Annual contract required before API access.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Project reads fully idempotent. Resource reads idempotent. Financial query reads idempotent by period. Timesheet reads idempotent. Project creation requires external ID for idempotency.
known_agent_gotchas: Mavenlink + Kimble merger creates documentation split — verify which API version applies
Annual contract required — significant commitment before production use
Resource management data is sensitive (utilization, billing rates) — restrict API access
Salesforce integration is certified and recommended over REST API for CRM-to-PSA sync
Rate limits not publicly documented — negotiate in contract for high-volume integrations

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. SOC2, GDPR, ISO27001 compliant. US, EU, UK data residency. OAuth2 with scopes. Financial data (billing rates, project costs, revenue) is highly sensitive — strict access controls required. SAML SSO for enterprise identity. Resource utilization and billing data must be protected from unauthorized access. Kimble (UK) acquisition adds UK GDPR compliance context.
